Field.Label: Network Scanner — discovers LAN devices via passive traffic sniffing, ARP sweep, ping and TCP probe. Creates one Indigo device per MAC address; tracks online/offline state, IP, vendor and open ports.
MAC OS Password used for "sudo" command
!!! your macOS login password — needed so tcpdump can open the raw network socket via sudo. !!! (Leave blank if tcpdump already has the required entitlements - unlikely.)
── The following settings should work as they are by default ──
Network Interface (primary)
List shows only interfaces that are currently UP and have an IP address. Auto-detect picks the first Ethernet/LAN port, falling back to Wi-Fi. The chosen interface is logged at plugin startup.
Network Interface (second, optional)
Optional second interface (e.g. Wi-Fi when Ethernet is primary). No extra threads or CPU when off
Scan Interval
How often to run the reachability probe (ping + curl TCP fallback) on known devices. Can be tuned per device in device edit.
Enable ARP Sweep
Sweep the subnet with ARP requests to discover new devices.
Enable Passive Traffic Sniffing

Passively capture all ethernet traffic via tcpdump to detect any active device — not just ARP. Catches devices that suppress ARP responses. Each MAC is throttled to one update per 5 s. Requires sudo password below if tcpdump does not already have the BPF entitlement.
Enable Active mDNS Query

Send an mDNS PTR query at the start of each ARP sweep to prompt all mDNS-capable devices to announce themselves. Useful for discovering devices hidden behind proxy-ARP access points (e.g. HomePod, AirPort Express). Responses are captured automatically by the passive sniff thread — no extra processing needed.
Offline Threshold
Mark a device offline if not seen for this many seconds, can be overwritten in device edit for each device.
Auto-Create Indigo Devices
Automatically create an Indigo device for each new MAC address found.
Create Synthetic Devices for Ping-Only Hosts

If an IP responds to ping but never appears in ARP (e.g. a device on a different VLAN), create an Indigo device for it using a synthetic MAC address (00:00:00:00:00:XX) and set it to ping-only mode. WARNING: some routers (proxy-ARP) answer ping on behalf of every IP on the subnet, which will flood this plugin with fake devices. Leave OFF unless you specifically need cross-VLAN monitoring.
Device Folder Name
Place auto-created Net_* devices in this Indigo folder (created if needed). Leave blank for root.
Variable Folder Name
Place plugin-created variables (e.g. networkScanner_newdevice) in this Indigo variable folder (created if needed). Leave blank for root.
Prefix Name
prefix for device name seg "NET_34:7E:5C:FC:B0:8E"
Show debug section
Most logging goes to the file: ... /Perceptive Automation/Indigo xx.y/Logs/com.karlwachs.networkscanner/plugin.log
Device tracking and per-device log controls are in the Plugin Menu → Track Device / Logging Tools.
Log New Device Created
Log when a new Indigo device is created for a discovered MAC.
Log Ignored MACs Skipped
Log each time an ignored MAC is seen and skipped.
Log Online / Offline Changes
Log when a device changes between online and offline.
Log IP Address Changes
Log when a device is seen with a different IP than before.
Log Every Device Seen
Log every ARP packet or sweep hit (can be noisy).
Log ARP Sweep Activity
Log when each ARP sweep starts and finishes.
Log Ping / Probe Results
Log every ICMP ping and TCP socket probe result to plugin.log (can be noisy during sweeps).
Log Tcpdump ARP Replies
Log every ARP reply captured by tcpdump (passive sniff), including throttled ones. Very noisy on busy networks — use for proxy-ARP / IP-churn diagnosis.
Log ARP Sweep Entries (arp -a)
Log every entry parsed from arp -a during each sweep cycle, with replied/cache-only status. Use to diagnose proxy-ARP deduplication.
